description: Content generation queue for TX Math Grades 3-8. Each task produces one module within a grade's course.json.

notes.skipStandards: Process standards (x.1), Personal Financial Literacy strands, and explain/describe-only standards are excluded.
notes.schema: Output must validate against validateCourse() from @primer/shared. See content/tx-math-g5/course.json for reference.
notes.existingContent: Grade 5 already has 2 modules in content/tx-math-g5/course.json — do not regenerate.

tasks.moduleDescription: Whole number place value through hundred thousands, composing and decomposing numbers, comparing and ordering. Unit fractions, number line representation, equivalent fractions, and comparing fractions.
tasks.skipReason: 3.3C and 3.3E are explanation/reasoning-only — no computable answer

tasks.moduleDescription: Fluency with add/subtract within 1000, multiply/divide within 100, one- and two-step problem solving, strip diagrams and equations, rounding to estimate.
Number patterns, input-output tables, equations with unknowns. Classifying 2D and 3D figures, decomposing composite figures, area and perimeter.
tasks.skipReason: 3.5C is qualitative description of multiplication/division expressions

tasks.moduleDescription: Time (nearest minute, elapsed time), liquid volume, mass, length. Customary and metric units. Frequency tables, dot plots, bar graphs.
tasks.skipReason: 3.7D is metacognitive (when to use which unit) — no computable answer

tasks.moduleDescription: Place value to billions, comparing/ordering whole numbers. Equivalent fractions, comparing fractions, decomposing fractions, adding/subtracting fractions with equal denominators, mixed numbers.
Recall facts to 12x12, multiply up to 4-digit by 1-digit, divide up to 4-digit by 1-digit, rounding and compatible numbers for estimation, multi-step problems with remainders.

Multi-step problems with strip diagrams, input-output tables, perimeter and area formulas. Points, lines, rays, angles, symmetry, classifying triangles and quadrilaterals. Measuring and drawing angles, unknown angle problems.
Relative sizes of units, converting within measurement systems, solving measurement problems. Frequency tables, dot plots, stem-and-leaf plots.

Classifying whole numbers, integers, rational numbers. Number opposites and absolute value. Ordering rationals on number lines. Integer and rational number operations (add, subtract, multiply, divide). Reciprocals.

Ratios as multiplicative comparisons, rates as division of different attributes. Representing ratios/percents with models, fractions, decimals. Benchmark percents. Equivalent forms. Unit rate conversions. Solving with scale factors, proportions, percent problems.
tasks.skipReason: 6.4A is qualitative comparison of additive vs multiplicative — no computable answer

tasks.moduleDescription: Independent and dependent quantities. Writing equations from tables (y=kx, y=x+b). Order of operations with exponents, prime factorization. Equivalent expressions using properties. Area formulas (parallelograms, trapezoids, triangles), volume of rectangular prisms. One-variable equations and inequalities.
tasks.skipReason: 6.9C is 'write a real-world problem' — open-ended, no computable answer

tasks.moduleDescription: Graphing in all four quadrants. Dot plots, stem-and-leaf plots, histograms, box plots. Mean, median, range, IQR. Categorical data with mode, relative frequency, percent bar graphs. Interpreting data distributions.
tasks.skipReason: 6.13B is conceptual (distinguish variability) — no computable answer

tasks.moduleDescription: Sets and subsets of rational numbers. Adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing rational numbers fluently. Multi-step rational number problems.

Constant rates of change (d=rt). Calculating rate of change from tables, graphs, equations. Constant of proportionality (k=y/x). Multi-step ratio, rate, and percent problems (including percent increase/decrease). Unit conversion. Similar shapes, scale drawings, pi as ratio of circumference to diameter.

Sample spaces (lists, tree diagrams). Experimental and theoretical probability for simple and compound events. Complement of an event. Random samples and inferences. Bar graphs, dot plots, circle graphs. Comparative dot plots and box plots.
tasks.skipReason: 7.6B is 'select simulations' (process); 7.12B is inferential reasoning — no computable answer

tasks.moduleDescription: Linear relationships (y=mx+b). Volume of prisms and pyramids. Circumference and area of circles. Composite figures. Surface area (rectangular/triangular prisms and pyramids). Two-step equations and inequalities. Angle relationships in triangles.
Sets and subsets of real numbers. Approximating irrational numbers (pi, square roots < 225). Scientific notation conversion. Ordering real numbers.

Similar shapes and dilations on coordinate plane. Slope via similar right triangles (m=rise/run). Graphing proportional relationships (y=kx). Rate of change and y-intercept from tables/graphs. Linear non-proportional (y=mx+b). Trend lines. Direct variation. Identifying functions. Writing linear equations.

Volume of cylinders, cones, spheres. Surface area formulas. Pythagorean theorem and its converse. Distance between points on coordinate plane. One-variable equations with variables on both sides. Systems of equations (graphical intersection).
tasks.skipReason: 8.6B/C are modeling/explaining; 8.8B is 'write a real-world problem'

tasks.moduleDescription: Rotations, reflections, translations, dilations on coordinate plane. Congruence vs similarity transformations. Algebraic representations of transformations. Scatterplots and association. Mean absolute deviation. Random sampling.
tasks.skipReason: 8.11C is conceptual (random sample representativeness) — no computable answer
